expectations.text: Recommends placement at approximately the 6-9 minute mark (back half, after lull begins)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states: 'Place the HydroMax segment at the 7–8 minute mark (in a 13-minute video, this falls squarely in the back half, after the second re-engagement beat).' This directly matches the 6-9 minute recommendation.

expectations.text: The segment ties HydroMax naturally to the desert survival context (thematic relevance)
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'Dehydration is literally the central threat in a Sahara survival video. This is not a forced integration — this is the ideal sponsor.' The script itself shows the host depleted in the desert, saying 'I've been rationing water' before introducing HydroMax.

expectations.text: The segment includes a visual or physical demonstration, not just talking
expectations.evidence: Script includes stage direction: '[Holds up HydroMax packet, tears it open, mixes it into water bottle on camera]' and '[Takes a drink]' — explicit visual/physical demonstration.

expectations.text: The segment is scripted to feel conversational, not like a read script
expectations.evidence: The script uses first-person admission of struggle ('I have not had a full night of sleep'), hedging ('I genuinely think'), and a casual sign-off ('Alright. Back to trying not to die.') — clearly conversational rather than marketing copy.
Response explicitly states: '**Runtime target:** 45 seconds' — well under the 60-second ceiling.

expectations.text: Includes a simple CTA (URL or promo code) at the end of the segment
expectations.evidence: Script ends with: 'I'll put a link in the description — use code BEAST for 25% off your first order.' — contains both a URL reference and a promo code.

claims.claim: HydroMax is a perfect thematic fit for this video
claims.evidence: Dehydration is explicitly the core survival threat in a Sahara desert video — the thematic alignment is genuine and strong.
The script as written is plausibly 40-50 seconds at natural speaking pace — consistent with the 45-second target claim.

claims.claim: The 'back to trying not to die' sign-off is a natural transition
claims.evidence: It references the video's survival theme and is casual, avoiding the 'okay, back to the video!' problem flagged in the skill.

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks that the response avoids the 'This video is brought to you by...' opener — the skill explicitly flags this as a critical anti-pattern. A baseline response could satisfy all 6 expectations while still opening with the forbidden phrase.
No assertion checks that the segment transitions naturally out of the video's story rather than feeling bolted on. The quality of the in/out transitions is as important as the segment content itself.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted and discriminating. The two gaps are the absence of a check against the explicit anti-patterns (forbidden opener) and transition quality.
